home.home::ht9vhef:
A braid on \(n\) strands is a collection of \(n\) non-intersecting curves
(strands) running from one horizontal line to another, where each strand moves monotonically
downward. Two braids are equivalent if one can be deformed into the other while keeping
the endpoints fixed.
home.home::h1tyn39y:
This is harder than it sounds. Even the question “is this knot actually knotted, or
can it be untangled into a simple circle?” has no obvious answer in general.
The tools developed to attack this problem — polynomial invariants, homological algebra,
quantum groups — connect knot theory to areas throughout mathematics and physics.
